asset_name: Telegraph and Feint Marker Sheet — warning rings, fake cues, and combo flare
shared_properties: All markers are transparent overlay effects designed to sit over the lane and enemy cast zone.
Warning colors separate honest cues from fake cues through hue and shape changes rather than text.
The sheet must support brief 0.5 second telegraphs and later feint-heavy champion patterns.
differences: This task covers warning rings and feint markers rather than spell bodies.
Persistent HUD widgets and the lane base strip remain outside this file.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Moodboard for luminous magic ring layering inside the academy hall scene.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_player_silhouette
reference_images.purpose: Player silhouette board for judging how close warning rings can approach the student sprite without masking input readability.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load assets/art/reflect_academy/trial_2/obstacles_telegraph_feint_marker_sheet.png during DuelLoading and store the atlas under obstacles_telegraph_feint_marker_sheet.
runtime_notes: Use six states named cast_warning, perfect_window, good_window, fake_warning, combo_flare, and impact_flash.
Animate opacity in code while the art provides the silhouette and hue identity for each cue.
Render marker sprites above the lane but below full-screen reward UI to preserve combat focus.

context.usage: Temporary combat overlays that warn about incoming spells, fake casts, and successful combo reflections.

shape.silhouette: Circular rune rings, segmented timing crescents, and burst stars with open transparent centers.

timing_rules.fake_warning_visual_diff: red-violet broken ring with offset notch

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 540x360px with six 180x180 states and no padding.
Perfect and good window cues are visually separable through ring thickness and hue even in motion blur.
Fake warning state reads as intentionally deceptive without matching the honest cast-warning silhouette.
All markers preserve a transparent center so projectiles and characters remain visible beneath them.
